name: Peanut Butter Chocolate Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Place sheets of foil on countertop for cooling cookies.
2. Place chocolate chips in a heavy resealable plastic bag or microwave-safe bowl. Microwave at high power for 30 seconds, then knead or stir and repeat if necessary until completely smooth.
3. Combine brown sugar, peanut butter, shortening, milk, and vanilla in a large bowl. Beat with an electric mixer on medium speed until well blended. Add melted chocolate and beat just until blended. Add egg and beat just until blended.
4. Combine flour, baking soda, and salt. Add to the creamed mixture at low speed. Mix just until blended. Stir in chopped peanuts.
5. Wrap dough in plastic wrap. Refrigerate for 3 to 4 hours or overnight. Keep refrigerated until ready to use.
6. Shape dough into 2-inch (5 cm) balls. Place cookies about 5 inches apart on an ungreased cookie sheet. Flatten into 3-inch (7 cm) patties. Mark each cookie in a cross-hatch pattern with the tines of a fork.
7. Bake one baking sheet at a time in the oven for 8 to 10 minutes, or until set and just beginning to brown. Do not overbake. Cool cookies on the sheet for 2 minutes before removing them to foil to cool completely.
Makes: About 3 dozen cookies
